questions.stem:
—______ you ever ______ to the Shenzhen Science Museum?
—Yes, I ______ there with my classmates last Sunday.
questions.explanation: 第一空和第二空考查现在完成时have been to（去过某地，已回来），ever是现在完成时的标志词；第三空有明确的过去时间状语last Sunday，必须用一般过去时went。A和D选项第一空用Did，但后面有ever，通常与现在完成时连用；C选项have gone to表示去了某地（未回），与答句不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ is lying on the desk is about environmental protection.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the book（物），定语从句中缺少主语，关系代词要用which或that。A选项who用于指人；B选项whom用于指人且在从句中作宾语；D选项whose表示所属关系，后需接名词。解题技巧：先行词是物，从句缺主语，选which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old textbooks ______ to the children in rural areas next week.
questions.explanation: 主语textbooks和动词send之间是被动关系（书被送），时间状语next week表示将来，所以用一般将来时的被动语态will be sent。A是一般现在时被动；B是一般过去时被动；C是主动语态。解题技巧：看到next week选将来时，主语是物选被动。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing on business.
questions.explanation: 根据答句He has gone to Beijing（他去北京了，还没回来），可知敲门的不可能是他。can't表示不可能（否定推测），符合语境。must be表示一定是；might be表示可能是；should be表示应该是。解题技巧：看到has gone to（去某地未回），用can't表示不可能。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are not ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: be satisfied with是固定搭配，表示对……满意。句意：如果你不满意现在的分数，你应该更努力。A选项strict常与with搭配，但表示对……严格；C选项angry常与at/about搭配；D选项patient常与with搭配，表示对……有耐心。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ of the old factory will cost a lot of money and time.
questions.explanation: construction（建造）由construct+ion构成，符合句意工厂建造。B选项instruction（指导）；C选项introduction（介绍）；D选项production（生产）。通过词根记忆：struct表示建造，con-表示一起，construction就是建造。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to reduce plastic waste in daily life.
questions.explanation: try one's best to do sth.是固定搭配，表示尽某人最大努力做某事。B选项make常与progress/mistake搭配；C选项do常与homework/exercise搭配；D选项take常与action/care搭配。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 前后句是因果关系：他们认为这是浪费时间，因为他们有太多作业要做。because引导原因状语从句。although表示让步；if表示条件；unless表示除非。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it was a waste of time (8)______ they had too much homework to do. However, after the teacher explained how pollution affects our daily life, more and more students joined in.

We collected plastic bottles and waste paper in special boxes. Every Friday afternoon, volunteers would (9)______ them to the recycling center. Although the work was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the (10)______ of our efforts. The money we got from selling the recyclables was used to buy books for children in rural areas.

This experience has taught us that even small actions can make a big (11)______. Now, I always think twice before throwing things away. I have learned to (12)______ things instead of buying new ones whenever possible.

questions.explanation: take...to...表示把……带到……（从说话者所在地带走）。句意：每周五下午，志愿者会把它们带到回收中心。send强调邮寄或派人送；bring表示带来（朝说话者方向）；carry强调搬运重物，不涉及方向。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it was a waste of time (8)______ they had too much homework to do. However, after the teacher explained how pollution affects our daily life, more and more students joined in.

We collected plastic bottles and waste paper in special boxes. Every Friday afternoon, volunteers would (9)______ them to the recycling center. Although the work was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the (10)______ of our efforts. The money we got from selling the recyclables was used to buy books for children in rural areas.

This experience has taught us that even small actions can make a big (11)______. Now, I always think twice before throwing things away. I have learned to (12)______ things instead of buying new ones whenever possible.

questions.explanation: the result of表示……的结果。句意：尽管工作很累，但当我们看到努力的结果时感到自豪。reason和cause都表示原因；problem表示问题，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it was a waste of time (8)______ they had too much homework to do. However, after the teacher explained how pollution affects our daily life, more and more students joined in.

We collected plastic bottles and waste paper in special boxes. Every Friday afternoon, volunteers would (9)______ them to the recycling center. Although the work was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the (10)______ of our efforts. The money we got from selling the recyclables was used to buy books for children in rural areas.

This experience has taught us that even small actions can make a big (11)______. Now, I always think twice before throwing things away. I have learned to (12)______ things instead of buying new ones whenever possible.

questions.explanation: make a big difference是固定搭配，表示产生重大影响。句意：这次经历教会我们，即使是小行动也能产生大影响。decision（决定）；difficulty（困难）；discussion（讨论）均不与make构成此搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it was a waste of time (8)______ they had too much homework to do. However, after the teacher explained how pollution affects our daily life, more and more students joined in.

We collected plastic bottles and waste paper in special boxes. Every Friday afternoon, volunteers would (9)______ them to the recycling center. Although the work was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the (10)______ of our efforts. The money we got from selling the recyclables was used to buy books for children in rural areas.

This experience has taught us that even small actions can make a big (11)______. Now, I always think twice before throwing things away. I have learned to (12)______ things instead of buying new ones whenever possible.

questions.explanation: reuse表示重复使用，与instead of buying new ones（而不是买新的）形成对比。reduce（减少）；recycle（回收）；repair（修理），虽然都与环保相关，但这里强调重复使用旧物而非购买新品。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it was a waste of time (8)______ they had too much homework to do. However, after the teacher explained how pollution affects our daily life, more and more students joined in.

We collected plastic bottles and waste paper in special boxes. Every Friday afternoon, volunteers would (9)______ them to the recycling center. Although the work was tiring, we felt proud when we saw the (10)______ of our efforts. The money we got from selling the recyclables was used to buy books for children in rural areas.

This experience has taught us that even small actions can make a big (11)______. Now, I always think twice before throwing things away. I have learned to (12)______ things instead of buying new ones whenever possible.

questions.stem: What did the scientists find about reading in the research?
options: A. Reading can improve memory immediately.
B. Reading fiction increases brain connectivity.
C. Reading is less useful than watching videos.
D. Reading only benefits language learning.
questions.explanation: 根据文章第二段A study...showed that people who read regularly...had increased connectivity in the left temporal cortex可知，科学家发现阅读能增加大脑颞叶皮层的连接性。A选项improve memory immediately（立即改善记忆）文中未提；C选项与原文相反；D选项only过于绝对。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Reading is not just a hobby; it is a powerful tool that can change our brains. Scientists have found that reading books, especially fiction, can improve our empathy and emotional intelligence.

When we read a story, our brains do not just process words. They create mental images and simulate the experiences of the characters. A study conducted by researchers at a university showed that people who read regularly for six months had increased connectivity in the left temporal cortex, an area associated with language and intelligence.

However, many teenagers today prefer watching videos or playing games to reading. They argue that these activities are more exciting. While digital entertainment has its value, it does not provide the same depth of thinking that reading offers. Reading requires active imagination, whereas watching videos is a more passive activity.

To encourage reading, some schools have started "reading challenges" where students compete to read the most books. The word "challenge" here does not mean something difficult or stressful. Rather, it refers to a friendly competition that motivates students to discover the joy of books.

Whether you prefer science fiction, history, or biography, the key is to read regularly. Just 30 minutes of reading before bed can significantly improve your sleep quality and vocabulary.

questions.stem: What can we infer about digital entertainment from the passage?
options: A. It is completely harmful to teenagers.
B. It requires more imagination than reading.
C. It offers a different kind of thinking depth.
D. It is more passive compared to reading.
questions.explanation: 根据第三段Reading requires active imagination, whereas watching videos is a more passive activity可知，看数字娱乐比阅读更被动。A选项completely harmful过于绝对；B选项与原文相反；C选项different kind of thinking depth文中未提，文中说it does not provide the same depth of thinking。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Reading is not just a hobby; it is a powerful tool that can change our brains. Scientists have found that reading books, especially fiction, can improve our empathy and emotional intelligence.

When we read a story, our brains do not just process words. They create mental images and simulate the experiences of the characters. A study conducted by researchers at a university showed that people who read regularly for six months had increased connectivity in the left temporal cortex, an area associated with language and intelligence.

However, many teenagers today prefer watching videos or playing games to reading. They argue that these activities are more exciting. While digital entertainment has its difficult or stressful. Rather, it refers to a friendly competition that motivates students to discover the joy of books.

Whether you prefer science fiction, history, or biography, the key is to read regularly. Just 30 minutes of reading before bed can significantly improve your sleep quality and vocabulary.

questions.stem: What does the word "challenge" in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
options: A. A difficult problem to solve.
questions.explanation: 根据第四段The word "challenge" here does not mean something difficult or stressful. Rather, it refers to a friendly competition可知，challenge在这里指友好竞赛。Rather表示转折，后面的内容就是对challenge的解释。A和C是通常含义，但文中明确说does not mean something difficult or stressful；D未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Reading is not just a hobby; it is a powerful tool that can change our brains. Scientists have found that reading books, especially fiction, can improve our empathy and emotional intelligence.

When we read a story, our brains do not just process words. They create mental images and simulate the experiences of the characters. A study conducted by researchers at a university showed that people who read regularly for six months had increased connectivity in the left temporal cortex, an area associated with language and intelligence.

However, many teenagers today prefer watching videos or playing games to reading. They argue that these activities are more exciting. While digital entertainment has its value, it does not provide the same depth of thinking that reading offers. Reading requires active imagination, whereas watching videos is a more passive activity.

To encourage reading, some schools have started "reading challenges" where students compete to read the most books. The word "challenge" here does not mean something difficult or stressful. Rather, it refers to a friendly competition that motivates students to discover the joy of books.

Whether you prefer science fiction, history, or biography, the key is to read regularly. Just 30 minutes of reading before bed can significantly improve your sleep quality and vocabulary.

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. The Disadvantages of Digital Entertainment
D. Ways to Start Reading Challenges
questions.explanation: 文章第一段点明主旨reading is a powerful tool，第二段讲阅读对大脑的影响，第三段对比阅读与数字娱乐，第四段讲阅读挑战，第五段总结阅读的好处。全文围绕阅读的力量展开。A选项只涉及细节；B选项只是对比部分；D选项只涉及第四段。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Reading is not just a hobby; it is a powerful tool that can change our brains. Scientists have found that reading books, especially fiction, can improve our empathy and emotional intelligence.

When we read a story, our brains do not just process words. They create mental images and simulate the experiences of the characters. A study conducted by researchers at a university showed that people who read regularly for six months had increased connectivity in the left temporal cortex, an area associated with language and intelligence.

However, many teenagers today prefer watching videos or playing games to reading. They argue that these activities are more exciting. While digital entertainment has its value, it does not provide the same depth of thinking that reading offers. Reading requires active imagination, whereas watching videos is a more passive activity.

To encourage reading, some schools have started "reading challenges" where students compete to read the most books. The word "challenge" here does not mean something difficult or stressful. Rather, it refers to a friendly competition that motivates students to discover the joy of books.

Whether you prefer science fiction, history, or biography, the key is to read regularly. Just 30 minutes of reading before bed can significantly improve your sleep quality and vocabulary.

questions.explanation: 前文说很多学生发现难以平衡学业和爱好，后文说通过良好的时间管理两者都可能实现，前后是转折关系，用However（然而）。Then表顺承；On the other hand表另一方面；Therefore表结果；In addition表递进。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework but still want to practice their hobbies. (17)______, it is possible to do both with good time management.

First, you need to make a schedule. Write down all your homework and assign specific times for each subject. (18)______, you will know exactly how much free time you have left for hobbies.

Second, choose hobbies that can be done in short periods. For example, reading or drawing can be done for just 20 minutes. (19)______, playing basketball or football usually requires more time and teammates.

Finally, remember that hobbies are important for your mental health. (20)______, you should not feel guilty about spending time on them. In fact, taking breaks can help you study better when you return to your books.

questions.explanation: 前文讲制定计划、写下作业、分配时间，后文讲你就会知道有多少空闲时间，这是顺承关系，用Then（然后）。However表转折；On the other hand表对比；Therefore表结果（因果关系）；In addition表递进。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework but still want to practice their hobbies. (17)______, it is possible to do both with good time management.

First, you need to make a schedule. Write down all your homework and assign specific times for each subject. (18)______, you will know exactly how much free time you have left for hobbies.

Second, choose hobbies that can be done in short periods. For example, reading or drawing can be done for just 20 minutes. (19)______, playing basketball or football usually requires more time and teammates.

Finally, remember that hobbies are important for your mental health. (20)______, you should not feel guilty about spending time on them. In fact, taking breaks can help you study better when you return to your books.

questions.explanation: 前文举例阅读或绘画可以短时间完成，后文说打篮球或踢足球通常需要更多时间和队友，前后是对比关系，用On the other hand（另一方面）。However表转折；Then表顺承；Therefore表结果；In addition表递进。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find it hard to balance their schoolwork and hobbies. They often feel stressed when they cannot finish homework but still want to practice their hobbies. (17)______, it is possible to do both with good time management.

First, you need to make a schedule. Write down all your homework and assign specific times for each subject. (18)______, you will know exactly how much free time you have left for hobbies.

Second, choose hobbies that can be done in short periods. For example, reading or drawing can be done for just 20 minutes. (19)______, playing basketball or football usually requires more time and teammates.

Finally, remember that hobbies are important for your mental health. (20)______, you should not feel guilty about spending time on them. In fact, taking breaks can help you study better when you return to your books.

questions.explanation: 前文说爱好对心理健康很重要，后文说你不应该为此感到内疚，前后是因果关系，用Therefore（因此）。However表转折；Then表顺承；On the other hand表对比；In addition表递进。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

Second, choose hobbies that can be done in short periods. For example, reading or drawing can be done for just 20 minutes. (19)______, playing basketball or football usually requires more time and teammates.

Finally, remember that hobbies are important for your mental health. (20)______, you should not feel guilty about spending time on them. In fact, taking breaks can help you study better when you return to your books.
